categories.name: Community and Commitments
categories.instructions: Complete one of the following courses.Transfer credit not accepted.

categories.name: Foundations of Christianity I
categories.instructions: Complete one of the following courses. One Foundations of Christianity course must be completed at Calvin University.

courses.title: Foundations in Biblical Literature and Theology

categories.name: Foundations of Christianity II
categories.instructions: Complete one of the following courses. One Foundations of Christianity course must be completed at Calvin University.

courses.title: Intermediate Studies in Biblical Literature and Theology

Augustine’s Confessions: A Map of the Heart

Jesus in the Gospels and Acts
The Gospel and Letters of John
Theology: From the Early Church through the Middle Ages
Theology: From the Reformation to the Present

categories.name: Health and Movement (Personal Fitness)
categories.instructions: Complete one of the following courses. A student participating at Calvin in a varsity sport for two full seasons is exempt. Completion of ROTC or other military training exempts a student from both Health and Movement requirements.

categories.name: Health and Movement (Leisure, Sport, and Skills)
categories.instructions: Complete one of the following courses. Completion of ROTC or other military training exempts a student from both Health and Movement requirements.

categories.name: World Languages I
categories.instructions: Complete one of the following courses. 102 courses have a prerequisite of 101. High school exemption from World Languages I is possible. To obtain a high school exemption, a student must complete three sequential years in the same language with a B or better for each semester (two years for students who are engineering or nursing majors at graduation). (Near) native speakers of a language other than English are exempt from World Languages I.

Complete coursework totaling at least 26 semester hours from at least five of the six Knowledge and Understanding categories, including at least 2 semester hours from each of the following categories: Arts, Oral Rhetoric, Visual Rhetoric; Humanities; Social and Behavioral Sciences. Students are encouraged to take 4 semester hour courses when possible.

categories.name: World Languages II
categories.instructions: Complete 0–4 semester hours of coursework numbered 201 or higher in a language other than English OR complete 101-102 (8 semester hours) in a different language from coursework completed for World Languages I.

courses.title: Advanced French I: Sustainability in France
Advanced Grammar, Composition and Conversation I

categories.name: Arts, Oral Rhetoric, Visual Rhetoric
categories.instructions: Complete coursework totaling 2–6 semester hours, with up to 4 semester hours in any one discipline. Headings below designate the disciplines.

courses.title: Music and Art in the Elementary Classroom
Music Theory Fundamentals in Global Context

categories.name: Engaged Citizenship Commitments
categories.instructions: Complete at least one tagged course in all three areas. Engaged Citizenship Commitment courses may overlap with other core requirements. Test credit, including Advanced Placement, CLEP, and International Baccalaureate, may not satisfy Engaged Citizenship Commitment requirements.
